newsSince: No material company-specific news since June 9. Backdrop remains a structural Swiss power-demand tailwind (data centers ~6-8% of Swiss electricity today, potentially up to 15% by 2030), but this is sector-level, not a Romande Energie-specific catalyst or contract. Price essentially flat (-0.62%).

sources.label: Can Switzerland's power grid keep up with its data centres? (SWI swissinfo)

verdicts.rankRationale: Test-time-scaling/electricity bucket: the utility's exposure to rising Swiss power demand (data centers toward 15% of grid by 2030) is intact but unchanged this period. No company-specific contract or guidance shift. Flat price, no new evidence; prior hold stands.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Swiss electricity demand growth driven by AI/data-center load
verdicts.primaryRisk: Regulated-utility growth is slow; demand tailwind accrues grid-wide, not to RE alone
verdicts.bull: Leading Western-Switzerland utility positioned for structural power-demand and renewable build-out.
verdicts.neutral: Steady regulated utility; AI/data-center power tailwind is real but diffuse and gradual.
verdicts.bear: Small-cap regulated utility with limited pricing leverage to the scaling-driven electricity theme.
